tool_input.summary: To investigate (and quantify) the elevation at which Venus volcanoes/coronae begin showing strong *low microwave emissivity* anomalies, the most directly relevant PDS holdings are Magellan radar radiometry–derived emissivity products (SCVDR/ARCDR/GVDR) combined with Magellan altimetry/topography. In this catalog snapshot I found PDS4 Magellan datasets for emissivity and topography; I did not find corresponding PDS3 SCVDR/ARCDR/GVDR dataset IDs.

candidates.title: Magellan SCVDR Emissivity Data File (EDF) Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Primary Magellan thermal microwave radiometry–derived surface emissivity measurements (the core observable behind 'low emissivity anomalies'). Use these emissivity values and compare against elevation to identify the onset elevation where low-emissivity becomes common over volcanoes/coronae.

candidates.title: Magellan Surface Characteristics Vector Data Record (SCVDR) Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Top-level bundle for SCVDR products (includes emissivity along with related derived scattering/altimetry reductions). Helpful for documentation and for linking emissivity files with geometry/ancillary SCVDR components.

candidates.title: Magellen ARCDR Radiometry Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Radiometry composites useful for mapping emissivity anomalies spatially and relating them to volcanic/corona features.

candidates.title: Magellen ARCDR Altimetry Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Altimetry/elevation data needed to determine the elevation threshold at which low-emissivity anomalies appear; pair with SCVDR/ARCDR radiometry.

candidates.title: Magellan GVDR Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Gridded summary products (Global Vector Data Record) derived from SCVDR inputs; useful for global/regional analyses of emissivity vs. elevation.

candidates.title: Magellan Venus Stereo-Derived Topography Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Independent/topography dataset for elevation context over volcanic edifices and coronae; can be used to confirm elevation breakpoints found in emissivity products.

dataset.title: Magellan Surface Characteristics Vector Data Record (SCVDR) Bundle
dataset.description: The Surface Characteristics Vector Data Record (SCVDR) is an orbit-by-orbit reduction of Magellan scattering and emission measurements carried out at Stanford University. The SCVDR includes near-nadir scattering functions obtained by numerical inversion from altimetry (ALT) echoes, results (e.g., rms surface slopes and Fresnel reflectivity) from fitting analytic functions to those inversions, scattering function segments at oblique incidence angles derived from synthetic aperture radar (SAR) echoes, and estimates of surface emissivity derived from thermal microwave radiometry (RAD) measurements. The SCVDR is one of several inputs to the Global Vector Data Record (GVDR), a gridded summary of scattering results, also produced at Stanford.
